capabilities.purpose: Emit a machine-readable view of every m subcommand (JSON)

options.name: --json
options.help: Emit JSON (currently the only supported format; accepted for explicitness)

coverage.purpose: Measure test coverage of an M project

options.name: paths
options.help: Project root or path(s) to scan (default: current directory)

options.name: --routines
options.help: Explicit production-routines path (repeatable). Skips auto-detect.

options.name: --tests
options.help: Explicit test-suites path (repeatable). Skips auto-detect.

options.name: --suites
options.help: Comma-separated suite names to restrict the run (default: all)

options.name: --format
options.help: Output format (default: text). 'lcov' emits a tracefile consumable by genhtml / Codecov / Coveralls.

options.name: --lines
options.help: Show line-level detail in text output. With --uncovered, also lists every uncovered executable line.

options.name: --uncovered
options.help: Print only uncovered labels (text format only)

options.name: --branch
options.help: Collect branch coverage: identify IF/ELSE/FOR/postconditional decisions and report which were reached during the run.

options.name: --min-percent
options.help: Fail with exit 1 if total coverage is below this percent

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress the summary line on stderr

doc.purpose: godoc-style symbol lookup over the m-stdlib manifest

options.name: symbol
options.help: Symbol to look up: MODULE, MODULE.label, or bare label name

options.name: --short
options.help: One-line synopsis instead of full long-form output

options.name: --json
options.help: Emit the raw manifest entry as JSON

options.name: --manifest
options.help: Path to dist/stdlib-manifest.json (default: walk up from cwd)

options.name: --format
options.help: Output format (default: text)

options.name: --fix
options.help: After running checks, invoke `m engine <verb>` for every WARN whose fix is an engine verb (install / start / ...). Non-engine fixes (sudo'd system commands) are NOT auto-run — a manual: line prints instead.

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Required to run destructive engine verbs (e.g. `reset`) via --fix. No-op without --fix.

errors.purpose: List every U-STD* error code and the labels that raise it

options.name: --json
options.help: Emit the errors index as JSON (the same shape as dist/errors.json)

options.name: --manifest
options.help: Path to dist/stdlib-manifest.json (default: walk up from cwd)

options.name: module
options.help: Module to scope output to (default: every module)

options.name: --manifest
options.help: Path to dist/stdlib-manifest.json (default: walk up from cwd)

options.name: paths
options.help: One or more .m files (or directories — searched recursively for *.m). Default: current directory.

options.name: --rules
options.help: Canonical-layout rules to apply: 'none' (identity, default), 'canonical' (SAC hygiene: trim + uppercase), 'pythonic' (expand abbreviations to canonical names: S→SET, $L→$LENGTH), 'pythonic-lower' (same but lowercase output: set, $length), 'compact' (inverse: SET→S, $LENGTH→$L), 'all' (every registered rule — diagnostic only), or a comma-separated list of rule ids. When unset, falls back to [fmt] rules from .m-cli.toml / pyproject.toml.

options.name: --check
options.help: Don't write; exit 1 if any file is not already formatted

options.name: --diff
options.help: Don't write; print unified diff for each file that would change

options.name: --stdout
options.help: Write formatted output to stdout (single-file mode)

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress per-file progress output

options.name: --list-rules
options.help: Emit the full fmt rule inventory (id, title, description, presets) as JSON and exit. Source of truth for dist/fmt-rules.json.

options.name: --json
options.help: Force JSON output. Currently only meaningful with --list-rules (the rule inventory always emits JSON in Phase 0); accepted for explicit invocation per the tier-1 manifest contract.

options.name: paths
options.help: One or more .m files (or directories — searched recursively for *.m). Default: current directory.

options.name: --rules
options.help: Profile name or comma-separated rule IDs (default: 'default', or [lint] rules from .m-cli.toml / pyproject.toml). See --list-profiles for the available named profiles.

options.name: --list-profiles
options.help: List the named lint profiles and exit

options.name: --list-rules
options.help: Emit the full rule inventory (id, severity, category, tags, profiles, fixer_id, description) as JSON and exit. Source of truth for dist/lint-rules.json.

options.name: --json
options.help: Force JSON output. Currently only meaningful with --list-rules (the rule inventory always emits JSON in Phase 0); accepted for explicit invocation per the tier-1 manifest contract.

options.name: --target-engine
options.help: Target M engine for engine-aware rules. 'any' (default) keeps the linter portable; 'yottadb' / 'iris' unlock engine-specific allowlists for $Z* ISVs/functions and Z-commands. CLI wins over [lint] target_engine in .m-cli.toml.

options.name: --threshold
options.help: Override a [lint.thresholds] config value. Repeatable. Example: --threshold line_length=120 --threshold routine_lines=2000. Known keys: line_length, code_line_length, routine_lines, label_lines (see m_cli.lint.thresholds.KNOWN_THRESHOLDS).

options.name: --format
options.help: Output format (default: text)

options.name: --error-on
options.help: Severity threshold for non-zero exit code: error | warning | style | info (default: warning)

options.name: --lint-unparseable
options.help: Lint files that have parse errors (default: skip them)

options.name: --jobs
options.help: Number of parallel worker processes (default: os.cpu_count(); 1 to disable the pool)

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress summary output

options.name: --fix
options.help: Apply auto-fixes for diagnostics whose rule has a `fixer_id`. Each unique fixer (an `m fmt` rule) runs once per affected file. Files are rewritten in place; remaining (non-fixable) findings are still reported. Combine with --check to preview without writing.

options.name: --baseline
options.help: Path to a baseline file (default: .m-lint-baseline.json in the first ancestor that contains one). Findings present in the baseline are suppressed. Use --update-baseline to regenerate.

options.name: --no-baseline
options.help: Disable baseline filtering even if .m-lint-baseline.json exists.

options.name: --update-baseline
options.help: Write current findings to the baseline file (default: .m-lint-baseline.json in the project root) and exit 0. Existing entries are replaced wholesale.

lsp.purpose: Run the m-cli Language Server (over stdio)

options.name: --verbose
options.help: Enable DEBUG-level logging on stderr

options.name: --rules
options.help: Rule filter for diagnostics — passed to `m_cli.lint.select_rules`. Examples: `default` (the built-in default profile), `all`, `xindex` (VA VistA Toolkit), `sac`, `M-XINDX-013,M-XINDX-019`.

manifest.purpose: Emit the m-stdlib manifest (or a sub-path) as JSON

options.name: path
options.help: Sub-path to emit (e.g. STDJSON.parse). Empty = whole manifest.

options.name: --manifest
options.help: Path to dist/stdlib-manifest.json (default: walk up from cwd)

options.name: name
options.help: Project name (also drives the M routine name)

options.name: --path
options.help: Target directory (default: ./<name>/)

options.name: --force
options.help: Scaffold even if the target directory exists and is non-empty

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress per-file progress output

options.name: --json
options.help: Emit the discovered set as JSON

run.purpose: Run an M routine via `ydb -run ENTRYREF`

options.name: entryref
options.help: ROUTINE or LABEL^ROUTINE to invoke (case-insensitive)

options.name: --routines
options.help: Path to prepend to $ydb_routines (repeatable). When unset, the parent env's $ydb_routines is used unchanged.

options.name: args
options.help: Extra arguments passed to the M program via $ZCMDLINE

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress the `m run: ydb -run ENTRYREF` banner

search.purpose: Full-text search over the m-stdlib manifest

options.name: query
options.help: Search query — space-separated tokens (AND-style match)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Max number of matches to print (default: 50)

options.name: --manifest
options.help: Path to dist/stdlib-manifest.json (default: walk up from cwd)

test.purpose: Run M test suites against YottaDB

options.name: paths
options.help: Files, directories, or `FILE::tLabel` selectors. With no argument, looks for `./routines/tests/`.

options.name: --list
options.help: List discovered suites and tests without running them

options.name: --filter
options.help: Only run suites whose name contains this substring

options.name: --format
options.help: Output format (default: text)

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress summary output

options.name: --changed
options.help: Run only suites whose source has changed in git (working tree + index + untracked). Combine with --changed-base to diff against a specific revision.

options.name: --changed-base
options.help: With --changed: diff against revision REV (e.g. main) instead of the working tree.

options.name: --no-isolation
options.help: Skip the per-test STDFIX transactional wrapper. Use for legacy ^TESTRUN-style suites that don't want a TSTART / TROLLBACK around each test (default: isolation on).

options.name: --seed
options.help: Load a STDSEED TSV manifest before running each test (`do load^STDSEED("PATH")`). Repeat for multiple seeds; order is preserved.

options.name: --env
options.help: Load a `.env` file via STDENV before running each suite. Parsed values land in `^STDLIB($JOB,"env",KEY)` so test code reads via `$get(^STDLIB($JOB,"env","KEY"))`. Repeat for multiple env files; later files override earlier keys.

options.name: --update-snapshots
options.help: Set the STDSNAP update sentinel before running each suite, so `asserts^STDSNAP` rewrites baselines instead of comparing. Run after an intentional change in test output to regenerate snapshot files.

options.name: --timings
options.help: Show per-suite wall-clock duration in the summary line. Captures the full subprocess invocation (SSH + ydb startup + test execution); useful for spotting suites that are slowing down the inner loop.

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Per-suite (or per-test, in single-test mode) timeout in seconds. The subprocess is killed if it runs past the deadline; the suite is reported as TIMEOUT, distinct from FAIL/0/0 caused by a parse-level zero-assertion result. Pass 0 to disable. Default: 600.

watch.purpose: Re-run M test suites on file change

options.name: paths
options.help: Files or directories to watch (default: ./routines/tests/)

options.name: --interval
options.help: Polling interval in seconds (default: 0.5)

options.name: --once
options.help: Run the initial pass and exit (no watch loop)

options.name: --filter
options.help: Only watch / run suites whose name contains this substring

options.name: --format
options.help: Output format (default: text)
